name: HookLaw
vision: Plataforma self-hosted de orquestração de webhooks com agentes AI e MCP tools. BYOK, single process Node.js, MIT.

tasks.title: Initialize project with package.json, tsconfig, and folder structure
tasks.description:
Create the HookLaw project scaffold:
- `package.json` with name hooklaw, type module, node >=20, scripts (build, start, dev, test)
- Dependencies: better-sqlite3, pino, pino-pretty, yaml, zod, commander, uuid
- DevDependencies: typescript, @types/node, @types/better-sqlite3, tsx, vitest
- `tsconfig.json` targeting ES2022, module NodeNext, outDir dist, strict true
- Create directory structure: src/, src/providers/
- Create empty entry files: src/index.ts, src/types.ts
- Add .gitignore (node_modules, dist, *.db, .env)
- Add .env.example with placeholder keys
acceptance: package.json exists with all listed dependencies
tsconfig.json exists with strict mode
Directory structure src/ and src/providers/ exist
Typecheck passes

tasks.title: Define core types and Zod schemas
acceptance: All types defined with Zod schemas
Schema validates a valid config object
Schema rejects invalid config (missing required fields)
Typecheck passes

hints: Export both z.object schemas and z.infer<> types

tasks.title: Implement config loader with YAML parsing and env substitution
tasks.description:
Create src/config.ts:
- loadConfig(path?: string): AppConfig — loads hooklaw.config.yaml from CWD or given path
- Parse YAML using 'yaml' package
- Substitute ${ENV_VAR} placeholders with process.env values
- Validate parsed result against Zod AppConfig schema
- Throw descriptive error if validation fails
- Export a getConfig() singleton that caches the loaded config
- Support dotenv: load .env file if present (use Node built-in --env-file or manual fs read)
- Write tests: valid config loads, missing env var warns, invalid config throws
acceptance: Loads and parses YAML config file
Substitutes ${ENV_VAR} with process.env values
Validates against Zod schema
Throws on invalid config with clear error message
Typecheck passes

hints: Load .env manually with fs if needed

tasks.title: Implement SQLite database layer with hooks and executions tables
tasks.description:
Create src/db.ts:
- initDb(dbPath?: string): Database — opens/creates SQLite DB, runs migrations
- Create tables: hooks (id TEXT PK, name, slug UNIQUE, enabled, mode, config_ref, created_at, updated_at) and executions (id TEXT PK, hook_id FK, status, payload, agent_output, tools_called, duration_ms, error, created_at)
- CRUD for hooks: createHook, getHook, getHookBySlug, listHooks, updateHook, deleteHook
- CRUD for executions: createExecution, updateExecution, getExecutionsByHook (with limit/offset), deleteOldExecutions(retentionDays)
- All functions take db instance as first param
- Write tests using in-memory SQLite (:memory:)
acceptance: Tables created with correct schema on init
CRUD operations work for hooks table
CRUD operations work for executions table
deleteOldExecutions removes entries older than retention
Typecheck passes

tasks.title: Create provider interface and Anthropic provider
acceptance: LLMProvider interface defined with chat method
AnthropicProvider implements interface
Tool definitions converted to Anthropic format correctly
Tool call responses mapped back to ChatResult
Typecheck passes

tasks.title: Implement OpenAI provider
tasks.description:
Create src/providers/openai.ts:
- Implements LLMProvider using openai SDK
- Maps ToolDefinition to OpenAI function format
- Handles function_call / tool_calls responses
- Maps back to ChatResult
- Add openai to package.json dependencies
- Write test with mocked SDK

Also works for OpenRouter (same API, different base_url).
acceptance: OpenAIProvider implements LLMProvider interface
Supports custom base_url for OpenRouter/Ollama
Tool definitions mapped to OpenAI function format
Typecheck passes

hints: OpenRouter uses same API as OpenAI with different base URL

tasks.title: Create provider factory and registry
tasks.description:
Create src/providers/index.ts:
- createProvider(name: string, config: ProviderConfig): LLMProvider
- Supports: 'anthropic' → AnthropicProvider, 'openai' → OpenAIProvider, 'openrouter' → OpenAIProvider (with base_url), 'ollama' → OpenAIProvider (with ollama base_url)
- Throws clear error for unknown provider
- Caches provider instances (singleton per provider name)
- Write test: factory creates correct providers, unknown throws
acceptance: Factory creates AnthropicProvider for 'anthropic'
Factory creates OpenAIProvider for 'openai', 'openrouter', 'ollama'
OpenRouter gets correct base_url
Unknown provider throws descriptive error
Typecheck passes

tasks.title: Implement agent executor with tool loop
tasks.description:
Create src/agent.ts:
- executeAgent(config: { provider: LLMProvider, model, temperature, system_prompt, payload: object, tools?: ToolDefinition[], onToolCall?: (name, args) => Promise<string> }): Promise<AgentResult>
- AgentResult: { output: string, tools_called: string[], duration_ms: number }
- Build messages: [system prompt, user message with JSON payload]
- Implement agentic tool loop: call LLM → if tool_calls, execute via onToolCall callback, feed results back → repeat until no more tool_calls or max iterations (10)
- Track all tools called
- Measure duration
- Write test with mock provider that returns tool calls then final answer
acceptance: Agent sends system prompt + payload to LLM
Tool loop executes tool calls and feeds results back
Stops after final text response (no tool calls)
Stops after max iterations to prevent infinite loops
Returns output, tools_called list, and duration_ms
Typecheck passes

tasks.title: Implement per-hook execution queue with concurrency control
tasks.description:
Create src/queue.ts:
- HookQueue class: manages a FIFO queue per hook slug
- enqueue(slug: string, task: () => Promise<void>): void
- Concurrency: 1 per hook (sequential processing per hook, parallel across hooks)
- Handles errors gracefully (logs, continues to next task)
- drain(): Promise<void> — waits for all queues to empty (for graceful shutdown)
- getQueueSize(slug: string): number
- Uses simple array + processing flag pattern (no external deps)
- Write tests: sequential execution per hook, parallel across hooks, error handling
acceptance: Tasks for same hook execute sequentially
Tasks for different hooks execute in parallel
Failed task does not block queue
drain() resolves when all queues empty
Typecheck passes

hints: Simple pattern: Map<string, { queue: Task[], processing: boolean }>

tasks.title: Implement HTTP server with webhook receiver endpoint
tasks.description:
Create src/server.ts:
- Uses Node.js built-in http module (no Express)
- POST /h/:slug — webhook receiver:
  - Parse JSON body
  - Look up hook by slug in config
  - If not found or disabled: 404
  - If mode async: respond 200 immediately, enqueue processing
  - If mode sync: process and respond with result
- GET /health — returns { status: 'ok' }
- createServer(config, deps: { db, queue, processWebhook }): http.Server
- Write test: health endpoint, webhook 404 for unknown slug, webhook 200 for valid slug
acceptance: GET /health returns 200 with status ok
POST /h/<valid-slug> returns 200 for async hooks
POST /h/<unknown-slug> returns 404
POST /h/<disabled-hook> returns 404
JSON body parsed correctly
Typecheck passes

hints: Use native http module, no Express

tasks.title: Implement webhook router that connects server → queue → agent → db
tasks.description:
Create src/router.ts:
- processWebhook(hookSlug: string, payload: object, deps: { config, db, queue, providers }): Promise<string | void>
- For async: enqueues a task that:
  1. Creates execution record in DB (status: pending)
  2. Updates to running
  3. Gets provider from factory
  4. Calls executeAgent with hook config + payload
  5. Updates execution with result (success + output, or error)
  6. Returns void
- For sync: same flow but awaits and returns agent output
- Logs each step with pino
- Write test with mock db, mock provider, verify execution lifecycle
acceptance: Creates execution record before processing
Updates execution status through lifecycle (pending → running → success/error)
Calls agent with correct system prompt and payload
Async mode returns void, sync mode returns output
Errors caught and stored in execution record
Typecheck passes

hints: This is the glue layer — keep it thin

tasks.title: Wire everything together in src/index.ts
tasks.description:
Create src/index.ts:
- startServer() function:
  1. Load config via loadConfig()
  2. Initialize SQLite DB via initDb()
  3. Create HookQueue instance
  4. Create HTTP server with webhook handler wired to router
  5. Start listening on configured port/host
  6. Log startup info (port, number of hooks, providers)
  7. Handle SIGINT/SIGTERM for graceful shutdown (drain queue, close db, close server)
- Export startServer for use by CLI
- Write integration-style test: start server, POST webhook, verify execution in DB
acceptance: Server starts and listens on configured port
Webhook POST creates execution in database
Graceful shutdown drains queue and closes resources
Typecheck passes

hints: Keep this file thin — just wiring

tasks.title: Implement CLI with init and start commands
tasks.description:
Create src/cli.ts:
- Use commander.js
- `hooklaw init`: creates hooklaw.config.yaml with example config, .env.example, initializes SQLite DB
- `hooklaw start`: calls startServer() from index.ts
- `hooklaw status`: loads config, connects to db, shows hook count, server config
- Set as bin entry in package.json: { "hooklaw": "dist/cli.js" }
- Add shebang to cli.ts: #!/usr/bin/env node
- Write test: init creates files, status outputs correctly
acceptance: hooklaw init creates config file and .env.example
hooklaw start boots the server
hooklaw status shows hook count and config
bin entry in package.json points to dist/cli.js
Typecheck passes

tasks.title: Implement CLI hook management commands
tasks.description:
Add to src/cli.ts:
- `hooklaw hook create <name>` — adds hook entry to config YAML file, registers in DB, prints URL
- `hooklaw hook list` — reads config, lists all hooks with status (enabled/disabled), slug, mode
- `hooklaw hook delete <name>` — removes from config YAML and DB
- `hooklaw hook test <name> --payload '{...}'` — sends a POST to localhost with given payload, shows result
- `hooklaw logs <hook-name>` — queries executions from DB, shows last 20 with status, duration, timestamp
- Write test: create adds to config, list shows hooks, logs queries DB
acceptance: hook create adds entry to YAML config and DB
hook create prints the webhook URL
hook list shows all hooks with status
hook delete removes from config and DB
hook test sends POST and shows response
logs shows recent executions
Typecheck passes

hints: Use yaml.stringify to write back to config file
hook test can use fetch() to POST to localhost

tasks.title: Implement MCP stdio client
tasks.description:
Create src/mcp-client.ts:
- Uses @modelcontextprotocol/sdk client
- connectStdio(config: McpServerConfig): Promise<McpConnection>
- McpConnection: { listTools(): ToolDefinition[], callTool(name, args): Promise<string>, disconnect(): void }
- Spawns child process with given command + args + env
- Connects via stdio transport
- Maps MCP tool schemas to LLMProvider ToolDefinition format
- Add @modelcontextprotocol/sdk to package.json
- Write test with a mock stdio MCP server (or test connection/disconnect lifecycle)
acceptance: Spawns process with correct command, args, env
Lists available tools from MCP server
Maps MCP tool schemas to ToolDefinition format
callTool sends request and returns result
disconnect kills child process
Typecheck passes

tasks.title: Implement MCP SSE client and multi-server manager
tasks.description:
Add to src/mcp-client.ts:
- connectSSE(config: McpServerConfig): Promise<McpConnection> — connects to remote MCP server via SSE transport
- connectMcpServers(configs: McpServerConfig[]): Promise<McpConnectionPool>
- McpConnectionPool: { getAllTools(): ToolDefinition[], callTool(name, args): Promise<string>, disconnectAll(): void }
- Pool aggregates tools from all connected servers, routes callTool to correct server
- Handle duplicate tool names across servers (prefix with server name if conflict)
- Write test: pool aggregates tools from multiple connections
acceptance: SSE client connects to remote MCP server
Pool aggregates tools from multiple servers
callTool routes to correct server based on tool name
disconnectAll cleans up all connections
Typecheck passes

tasks.title: Wire MCP tools into agent execution in router
tasks.description:
Update src/router.ts to:
- Before calling executeAgent, connect to MCP servers configured for the hook
- Pass MCP tools as ToolDefinition[] to agent
- Pass onToolCall callback that routes to McpConnectionPool.callTool
- After execution, disconnect all MCP connections
- Handle MCP connection errors gracefully (log warning, continue without tools)
- Support mcp_presets: if hook references a preset name, resolve from config.mcp_presets
- Write test: webhook with MCP tools triggers tool calls through pool
acceptance: MCP servers connected before agent execution
Agent receives tools from MCP servers
Tool calls routed through MCP connection pool
MCP connections cleaned up after execution
MCP connection failure does not crash execution
Preset resolution works
Typecheck passes

hints: Connect MCP → get tools → pass to agent → disconnect

tasks.title: Implement REST API endpoints for hook management
tasks.description:
Add to src/server.ts:
- POST /api/hooks — create hook (body: { name, mode?, agent config })
- GET /api/hooks — list all hooks
- GET /api/hooks/:id — get hook details
- DELETE /api/hooks/:id — delete hook
- PATCH /api/hooks/:id — update hook config
- GET /api/hooks/:id/logs — get executions (query: limit, offset)
- POST /api/hooks/:id/test — trigger test execution with provided payload
- Auth: if config.server.auth_token set, require Authorization: Bearer <token>
- All responses as JSON
- Write test: CRUD operations, auth check
acceptance: All CRUD endpoints work correctly
Auth token validated when configured
Missing auth returns 401
Logs endpoint returns paginated executions
Test endpoint triggers webhook processing
Typecheck passes

hints: Keep using native http, add URL pattern matching

tasks.title: Add built-in MCP preset configs for popular servers
tasks.description:
Create src/presets.ts:
- Export DEFAULT_PRESETS: Record<string, McpPreset> with configs for:
  - github: @modelcontextprotocol/server-github (GITHUB_TOKEN)
  - slack: @modelcontextprotocol/server-slack (SLACK_BOT_TOKEN)
  - notion: @modelcontextprotocol/server-notion (NOTION_API_KEY)
  - linear: mcp-linear (LINEAR_API_KEY)
  - filesystem: @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem
  - postgres: @modelcontextprotocol/server-postgres (DATABASE_URL)
- mergePresets(configPresets, defaultPresets): merged presets (config overrides defaults)
- resolvePreset(name, presets): McpServerConfig — resolves a preset name to full config, validates required env vars exist
- Write test: resolve preset, merge logic, missing env var warning
acceptance: Default presets defined for 6+ popular MCP servers
Config presets override defaults
resolvePreset returns full McpServerConfig
Missing required env var throws descriptive error
Typecheck passes

hints: Presets are convenience — user can always configure manually

tasks.title: Implement log retention cleanup and startup log purge
tasks.description:
Add to src/db.ts:
- cleanOldExecutions(db, retentionDays): number — deletes executions older than retention, returns count deleted

Add to src/index.ts:
- On startup, run cleanOldExecutions with configured retention_days
- Log how many old executions were purged
- Optionally set interval to run cleanup daily (setInterval 24h)

Write test: create old executions, run cleanup, verify deletion
acceptance: Old executions deleted based on retention_days
Cleanup runs on startup
Count of purged records logged
Recent executions not affected
Typecheck passes

hints: Use SQLite datetime comparison: created_at < datetime('now', '-30 days')

tasks.title: Write README.md and example config file
tasks.description:
Create README.md:
- Project description and value prop (1 paragraph)
- Quick start: install, init, configure, start (4 commands)
- How it works: webhook → agent → MCP tools diagram
- Config reference: server, providers, hooks, mcp_servers, mcp_presets, logs
- CLI reference: all commands with examples
- API reference: all endpoints
- BYOK section: supported providers
- MCP presets: list of built-in presets
- Example use cases: GitHub PR review, Stripe payment notification, form submission processing

Create hooklaw.config.example.yaml:
- Full example config with comments explaining each section
- Two example hooks (github-pr-review, stripe-payment)

Create LICENSE (MIT)

tests.command: test -f README.md && test -f hooklaw.config.example.yaml && test -f LICENSE

acceptance: README has quick start, config reference, CLI reference, API reference
Example config has two working hook examples with comments
MIT LICENSE file exists
All CLI commands documented

hints: Keep README concise and copy-paste friendly
Example config should work if user fills in API keys
